_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for Astera Labs (ALAB). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: Wisesheets — not yet pulled. Agent must search for TTM financials before running DCF.
_note: ALAB IPO March 2024. Limited financial history. DCF requires conservative assumptions given early stage.
story_narrative: Astera Labs makes the connectivity chips (PCIe/CXL retimers, optical interconnects) that tie together the components inside an AI server and between servers. Aries PCIe retimers allow longer distances and higher speeds inside the rack; Leo optical interconnects allow rack-to-rack bandwidth at scale. The structural opportunity is CXL memory pooling — disaggregating memory from CPU/GPU and pooling it across machines. If CXL becomes the memory architecture for AI data centers (Microsoft and Meta are piloting), ALAB is the connectivity fabric. Atreides Management (Gavin Baker) holds $369M in ALAB — their top position. The risk: revenue is currently dominated by PCIe retimers sold into NVIDIA GPU servers; if NVIDIA changes server architecture, demand could shift quickly. CXL is a 2027+ opportunity still.
story_revenue_cagr: 35% from a small base. Aries retimers ship into every high-end AI server; H100/Blackwell adoption drives near-term. CXL provides the 2027–2030 growth layer. Very high CAGR but small absolute base makes it achievable.
story_target_margin: 28% EBIT margin as scale is reached. Currently pre-profitability or early profits. Fabless model (manufactured at TSMC) allows high margins at scale like other connectivity chip companies.
story_growth_years: 10 years — PCIe retimer adoption (2024–2027) → CXL memory pooling (2027–2030) → next connectivity standard. ALAB is playing the infrastructure transition.
story_stable_growth: 3% — mature connectivity chip market grows with server unit volume. CXL by then is standardized infrastructure.
story_stable_margin: 22% stable EBIT — connectivity chips commoditize over time as the standard matures; margins compress from peak.
story_s2c_high: 2.5x — fabless, relatively capital-light. Main investment is in design engineering talent and TSMC NRE costs for new chips.
story_s2c_stable: 2.8x — ongoing improvement as chip designs are reused across customers and software-defined connectivity features add recurring revenue.
story_beta: 1.90 — very high beta for an early-stage, single-market company. Revenue concentration in AI server BOM and CXL timing risk justify high beta.
story_rd_cap: 5-year amortization. PCIe/CXL protocol IP has useful life tied to the spec generation (~3–5 years). Atreides' conviction partly reflects undervalued R&D IP.
story_uncertainty: High — single-market revenue concentration (NVIDIA GPU servers), CXL timing is uncertain (standard still maturing), and very limited public financial history. Requires widest MoS of all 7 picks.
